conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official guidance to ensure I can give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children? If yes, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's 6 years old and lives with me here in the UK. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! It's helpful to know about Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions. Let me start with:

**Are you responsible for Alex's care? By this I mean, do you live with him and/or pay for his expenses such as food, clothes, or other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I take care of all his needs - his food, clothes, school things, everything really. I'm his parent and he's at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. It sounds like you're responsible for Alex's care.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or has he been for an extended period recently?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not at all. Alex is at home with me - he hasn't been in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
- You live with him in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for his care and pay for all his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He is not in hospital or care

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for Alex.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit regardless.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for his care, paying for all his needs including food, clothes, and school things. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and only one person (the claimant) is claiming for this child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 6), who lives with them in the UK. The claimant provides for all of Alex's needs, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and he is not in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria are met for Child Benefit.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
